content:
First one shows by similarity that in fact 
\[
AB=4,\;BC=5,\;CA=6\quad\Longrightarrow\quad AD=\frac{CA^2}{AB}=\frac{36}{4}=9,\;
CD=\frac{3}{2}\,BC=7.5.
\]
Putting 
\[
A=(0,0),\;B=(4,0),\;C\bigl(3.375,\;4.96078\bigr)
\]
one finds by the complex‐number similarity map 
\[
z_D=\frac{z_C}{4}\,z_C\;=\;\frac{z_C^2}{4}
\]
that 
\[
D\bigl(-3.3047,\;8.3721\bigr).
\]
Parametrize the ray \(D\to A\) past \(A\) by 
\[
P=A+t(A-D),\;t>0.
\]
Imposing 
\(\angle BDC=\angle ACP\) leads to a single quadratic in \(t\), whose positive root is approximately \(t=0.4445\).  Then
\[
DP^2\;=\;\bigl|P-D\bigr|^2
\;\approx\;(4.7727)^2+( -12.0951)^2
\;\approx\;169.
\]
In fact one checks exactly that 
\[
DP=13
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\boxed{169}.
\]
